title: Health & Medicine Checklist
description: Prepare your health essentials for visiting China

items.detail: Recommended vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid. Consider Hepatitis B, Japanese Encephalitis, and Rabies based on itinerary
Ensure policy includes emergency medical evacuation, hospital stays, and covers China specifically
Confirm insurance covers pre-existing conditions, adventure activities if planned, and has 24/7 assistance in China
Obtain doctor's letter listing all medications, dosages, and medical necessity in English and Chinese
Keep medications in original containers, bring extra supply, and pack in carry-on luggage
Include: antiseptic wipes, bandages, pain relievers, anti-diarrhea medicine, rehydration salts, insect repellent
Cards in English and Chinese listing food allergies, drug allergies, and emergency treatment instructions
Carry card with blood type, Rh factor, and any special medical conditions in both languages
Summarize chronic conditions, past surgeries, hospitalizations, and current treatments in both languages
Include: local embassy, insurance emergency line, family contacts, and China emergency number (120 for ambulance)
Monitor AQI for destinations, consider packing N95 masks if sensitive or visiting northern industrial cities
Note that TCM is widely available; understand it's different from Western medicine; have insurance details ready
